course_full_title: Codes and Standards Definitions and History
lecture_topics: three sources of wealth: agriculture, mining, manufacturing
history of codes: Hammurabi, Hippocratic Oath, Magna Carta
Ben Franklin and lightning protection / NFPA 780
CSST tubing failures from lightning strikes
Franklin Institute and the first federal research contract (steamship boiler explosions)
ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code history (Brockton shoe factory explosion)
Galloping Gertie (Tacoma Narrows) and Petroski's 'To Engineer is Human'
Middletown CT 2010 gas explosion and NFPA 56
ASTM A106 / ASME B31.1 refinery pipe dispute (hydrostatic vs NDT testing)
code interpretations process and code committee structure
fair use of codes and standards

John Godfrey Saxe (1872 poem, 'The Blind Men and the Elephant')

flags_for_editorial_review: NFPA 780 thickness threshold: captioner produced both '3/8 inch' and '3/16 inch' in same paragraph; normalized to 3/16 per standard.
B31.1 subsection reference at L40:00 may have been '311.32' rather than 'B31.1.2'; bracketed alongside.
X-15 builder: Tom says 'Bell developed the X-15' — actually North American Aviation built the X-15; Bell built the X-1. Preserved per convention §16 (Tom's genuine misstatement).
